action.reasoning: Observation: the first baseline failed quickly with KeyError 'all' when fetching Alpha158 data, so the pipeline issue is in Qlib data access rather than model training. Analysis: Alpha158.fetch(col_set='all') is not valid for this handler/version; the robust approach is to use handler.fetch() default output and flatten MultiIndex columns, then explicitly separate feature/label columns by top-level names. Decision: patch train.py and predict.py to load data correctly, infer feature columns from non-label columns, run a short baseline training, locally test predict.py, and submit eval immediately to validate the end-to-end pipeline and get a runtime/score baseline.
action.command: python train.py --ckpt_dir checkpoints/baseline && mkdir -p tmp_eval && printf '{"test_period": ["2021-01-01", "2021-12-31"], "universe": "csi300", "feature_set": "Alpha158"}' > tmp_eval/eval_config.json && python predict.py --data_path tmp_eval --checkpoint checkpoints/baseline --output tmp_eval/out.json && python -c "import json;print(json.load(open('tmp_eval/out.json'))['ic_mean'])"
